page: Amazon Web Services i8ge.metal-24xl is a storage-optimized bare-metal server powered by 96 dedicated physical cores of the 64-bit ARMv9 AWS Graviton architecture (Neoverse-V2) running at 2.8 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the system features 60,000 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 150 Gbps. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance type.

In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier performance in multi-core processing, including stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression. Memory subsystem performance is also in the top tier, with high read and write bandwidths across both small and large blocks. Database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput also rank in the top ten percent. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench image processing scoring in the average tier.

While the server excels in multi-threaded tasks and database operations, it exhibits mixed results in language model inference. Text generation for a small 135M model ranks in the bottom ten percent, whereas text generation for medium 7B and large 70B models reaches the top twenty-five percent. Given the massive 60,000 GB local NVMe storage, high memory bandwidth, and top-tier multi-core capabilities, this server is configured for large-scale databases, high-throughput web serving, and data-intensive storage workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.metal-24xl is a storage-optimized bare-metal server featuring 96 dedicated physical cores on the 64-bit ARMv9 AWS Graviton architecture running at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory and 60,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, alongside a 150 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show top-tier performance in multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, database operations, and static web serving. Conversely, single-core performance and small-model text generation benchmarks are average to poor. This resource density makes the server highly efficient for large-scale database systems, multi-threaded data processing, and high-throughput storage workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.metal-24xl is a storage-optimized bare-metal server with 96 ARMv9 Graviton cores, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 60,000 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.metal-24xl is a storage-optimized server with 96 ARMv9 cores, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 60,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A storage-optimized bare-metal platform combining ninety-six ARMv9 cores with sixty terabytes of local NVMe SSD storage for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated ARMv9 AWS Graviton physical cores running at 2.8 GHz
60,000 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
768 GB of DDR5 memory with top-tier read and write bandwidth
Top-tier multi-core, database, and static web serving benchmark performance
Best suited for large-scale databases and high-throughput storage workloads
